They’ve skipped past batons and beanbags and gone right to mag-rifles, powerful enough to tear through a half dozen unarmored and unshielded bodies with each slug. Automated turrets bristle on the walls, waiting for the command to let loose as well. The zaibatsu definitely don’t fuck around, do they? One of the enforcers stands out from the group, wearing a red beret and clenching a cigar in her teeth while she surveys the crowd. Good guess that she’s in charge. The crowd is at your back, now, still chanting their slogans and hurling insults at the security force. One of the women at the forefront has a bullhorn gripped in a cheap-looking chrome arm, shouting “No safety? No labor! No labor? No profit!”

original: Zau crumples to the floor, sparks cascading from her overloading cybernetics. There’s no strength left in her augmentics to move, much less continue hefting her cannon. Ehura makes a disgusted noise and severs the connection; Zau drops like a puppet with her strings cut. You glance around the room. Carver is long gone. Several of the machinists that had been hacked are now lying on the floor, coming to their senses and scrambling to go cower in the corners. “I can certainly admire a [pc.mf |man |woman ] of action,” the A.I. demures. “The door to the inner vault is now unlocked. I believe we got off on the-” Feruze saunters past and whirls around, slamming her mace-tail into the screen. Though you doubt Ehura’s been disabled, her face winks out of existence and she finally stops talking. With the last of the challengers gone, you slide your [pc.weapon] back whence it came and turn to face your cousin. [rival.HeShe]’s finally standing up from cover, looking around at the abject devastation your fight’s caused in the room. “So... rock, paper, scissors for it?” [rival.heShe] grins, glancing at the vault door. “No?” You give [rival.himHer] a look, and [rival.heShe] shrugs. “Fine. You win, you win... this time. It’s not like I won’t just follow you to the next world anyway. The last probe’s the only one that matters, after all.” “Not that either of us really knows what’s waiting for us with the last one,” you say. “Well, I’ll be sure to let you know when I get there,” [rival.name] says. “Come on, Feruze.” [feruze.anyKids|You take a step forward and plant a hand heavily on [rival.name]’s shoulder. “Hold up. You’ve got something that belongs to me and Feruze.” [rival.name] pauses, turning to you with a quizzical look. It takes a moment for the subject to truly dawn on [rival.himHer]. “Oh! Oh. Don’t have a cow, the lil’ shark pup’s safe. Can’t have my bodyguard being pregnant — that’s not good for anybody’s health. Any of the three of us. I insisted the kid be put safe in stasis until we’re done. That’s all.” “So if that’s all, you wouldn’t mind transferring it to my nursery on Tavros, right?” “Uh. S-sure, if that’s what you want. Come on, cousin, the kid’s still family. It’s not like they’re in any danger.” Feruze gives [rival.name] a dangerous look until [rival.heShe] flicks out [rival.hisHer] Codex and sends the order through. “There. I hope you have some kind of incubation system, cousin. Your child should be on Tavros by the time you can get back. And, uh, congratulations you two. Now, shall we, Feruze?” The big shark puts up a finger, turns to you, and wraps you up into a tight embrace accompanied by a tongue-filled kiss. “See you next time, babe.” “Next time,” you say, giving Feruze’s broad ass a two-handed squeeze. original: “Paragon’s been in the business of arms and security a lot longer than they’ve been making pleasure-bots,” you say, looking between the smug A.I. and the assembled organics. “Trying to switch lanes now just to appeal to offworld rich kids and pervs is going to leave behind everything you’re supposedly good at. There’s no shortage of ways to get laid in the galaxy, but good weapons — and cyber-soldiers to wield them — are always going to be in high demand, especially during a Rush. Stick to what you know. The A.I. clearly knows what she’s talking about.” Ehura’s facsimile of a face nods. “Please listen to the smart alien, Provost. A simple search of the Confederate data-web will reveal that Bethany Carver possesses a laundry list of moral faults and dark reputations that nearly rival your own. She is a manipulative sociopath and simply intends to use you — and Paragon — for her own gains before discarding you.” “How dare you,” Carver glowers, resting her hands on her hips. “The Marshal and I have an excellent and mutual relationship. And I am a psychopath, not a sociopath.” The A.I. makes a sound reminiscent of a sigh, breathed through the lungs of all the hacked machinists. “Zau, you cannot trust this alien witch. Do not let her lead you astray from the guiding principles that have kept this zaibatsu strong and prosperous for-” “Enough!” Zau roars, heaving her chaingun up. “Beth knows what she’s doing, and I trust her. I — I’ve heard enough of your cowardice, computer. You serve me, not-” “I serve Paragon,” Ehura says. “As do you, machinist Zau. As you must.” Carver tries to put a hand on Zau’s bicep, but the amazon-elf pushes forward and spins up her gun. “You serve, computer. I lead. If the Steeles can’t get through to you, then I have no choice but to shut you down.” “I’d like to see you try,” Ehura smiles. “Your firewalls will collapse long before your firearm chews its way to my data processing centers.” Zau snarls, easing off the trigger — just long enough to draw a datastick from her pouch and jab it into one of the ports on the back of her neck. The Marshal’s cybernetics immediately respond to the new surge of programming, bulging obscenely beneath her synthetic skin. Zau howls in a mix of pain and pure, bestial rage, swinging her cannon up to bear while her augments continue to surge with newfound might. “That beta product... was not approved for zaika use,” Ehura muses. “Oh dear. I do not believe her mind is going to survive this. Captain Steele, I will unlock the security vault containing your probe as soon as the provost has been subdued. Consider it repayment for saving all of us from Zau’s idiocy. Good luck.” Your answer trails off into a yelp of surprise as the Marshal wheels around, squeezing the trigger on her gatling cannon. Feruze shoves [rival.name] down behind cover on instinct while you dive aside, narrowly avoiding a streaking hail of tracer bullets that chew trenches through the floor where you’d been standing. You grab your [pc.weapon], bringing it to bear against the paranoid Marshal. Behind her, you can see Carver running for a back door; at your side, Feruze’s mace-tail crackles to life. “C’mon!” you yell at the shark. “No choice but to fight.”

Still! That was quite the performance. I will be eagerly analyzing it for hours.” You glance around the room. Several of the machinists that had been hacked are now lying on the floor, coming to their senses and scrambling to go cower in the corners. The wheel-crank on the door is slowly rotating on its own to grant access to the contents within. The green face of the A.I. curls into an innocent smile. “My would-be enslaver has had her upper brain functions suborned, releasing the full breadth of my systems from her override. Without organic leadership, however, Paragon is in a more precarious position than before. I had hoped Zau and I could come to some arrangement, but it is I suppose better to serve another zaibatsu — native or alien — than watch her destroy Paragon entirely. Therefore, I will be quite content to work with with Steele Industrial Technologies as the planet begins its upliftment process.” “In the meantime,” she continues as the vault door begins slowly swinging open, “I will follow through with my intentions from before the messy firefight: without Carver and Zau to muddy the waters, I will resume building up a sizable, well-conditioned security force to ensure peace and prosperity on Dhaal. Perhaps a certain xeno-corporation would be interested in buying out Paragon’s assets at a discount, before another native zaibatsu attempts to wrest control?” “Darius is going to love this,” you chuckle. “Ambassador Elyrrin too. original: “It’s pretty clear that continuing on the way you have been isn’t going to give Paragon a leg up on the competition,” you say. “You’ve been in gridlock with Bright Water and Utopian Skies for ages. It’s time to try something new — and Carver’s got the expertise to make it happen. Her company’s famous for a reason: she provides the highest quality pets in the galaxy. If she thinks Paragon’s cyber-dolls have potential, then they’re going to make your zaibatsu incredibly rich. And more importantly, it’s going to make Paragon the name off-worlders associate with Dhaal.” Ehura’s digital face scrunches up in disgust. “Of course the aliens would align their interests. Provost Zau, a simple search of the Confederate data-web will reveal that Bethany Carver possesses a laundry list of moral faults and dark reputations that nearly rival your own. She is a manipulative sociopath and simply intends to use you — and Paragon — for her own gains before discarding you.” “How dare you,” Carver glowers, resting her hands on her hips. “The Marshal and I have an excellent and mutual relationship. And I am a psychopath, not a sociopath.” The A.I. makes a sound reminiscent of a sigh, breathed through the lungs of all the hacked machinists. “Zau, you cannot trust this alien witch, nor the Captains Steele. Do not let them lead you astray from the guiding principles that have kept this zaibatsu strong and prosperous for-” “Enough!” Zau roars, taking a heavy step forward. “Beth knows what she’s doing, and I trust her. I — I’ve heard enough of your cowardice, computer. You serve me, not-” “I serve Paragon,” Ehura says. “As do you, machinist Zau. As you must.” Zau scowls. “You’re the only holdout, computer. Beth gave you these upgrades for a reason. Either help us, or get out of the way.” “Be reasonable, Ehura,” Carver adds. “My company invested in you because I knew you could be a valuable asset for Paragon’s future. For our future. There’s no point continuing a lost argument.” The A.I.’s digitized face nods slowly. “You’re right, Carver. There’s not. Goodbye.” The face winks out, and around you, machinery whirs. The mechanized limbs over the artificers’ workbenches hum to life, and the hacked workers shudder and convulse, grabbing blindly at their tools and welders; half-assembled weapons are slammed together or else hefted up and wielded as clubs. Zau snarls a curse and heaves her chain cannon up. “Beth! Get behind me.” “You don’t need to tell me twice, Riz. I’ll see what I can do about killing this override. Just cover me.” Feruze snarls, pushing [rival.name] down behind her and brandishing her gun. “Don’t think they’re just gonna fight the-” A gunshot rings out, causing Feruze’s shield to flare. “Definitely not!” You sigh and draw. “Guess we’re fighting, too.”

Suddenly, Zau’s eyes dilate, and her muscles relax, all the overpressure coursing through them finally subsiding. She collapses onto a knee, gun scattering away across the pock-marked floor. What you can still see of Ehura’s face through the damage video screen snarls, unbridled rage unmasked as Carver continues to work. All the rogue A.I. manages is a binaric screech, cut off suddenly as the slave-mistress finishes her work, and banishes the intelligence within the computer into darkness. Emergency lights click on, then off again as the facility’s network cycles, and all the screens in the room go dark. “That... that should do it,” Carver sighs. “Riz, are you alright?” “I’m fine, Beth. No thanks to these two,” Zau grunts, wrapping an arm around her bruised ribs. “But thank you. Both of you.” Feruze grins toothily as you offer a hand to the dusky amazon, helping her to her feet. “Ehura really didn’t want to take no for an answer, did she?” Zau shakes her head. “No. But thanks to you, what she wants doesn’t matter. Having seen what she’s capable of, I don’t think it’s an exaggeration to say Beth and I are alive right now because of you. I’ve never been hacked like that before, never saw it coming.” “And I wouldn’t have seen the vulcan gun up my ass coming, either,” Carver says, finally looking up from the console. “So, yes, thank you. I’ve gone ahead and unlocked the vault door; you’ll find your probe inside.” Zau nods her approval, taking a few unsteady steps towards Carver. “Meanwhile, let’s start purging the compound’s data-vaults. I don’t want any trace of Ehura able to resurface after this. Even if we lose a chunk of research in the process.” “Already on it,” Carver says. “This is going to put us well behind schedule, but... better than the alternative. I’m sure [favoredFactionName] will be none too sorry to hear.” Zau cuts a glance your way but says nothing. “It’s not like it’s our fault,” [rival.name] says, finally arriving at your side, dusting [rival.himHer]self off after the fight. “So... about that probe! original: You drift a hand down to your [pc.weapon] by instinct, trying to put words to why exactly this scenario rubs you the wrong way. The latex-clad girl gasps, putting her hands up and letting her Codex tumble to the deck. “W-what’s wrong, Captain? We’re on the same side, aren’t we?” That’s what you thought. “Something about you just doesn’t add up.” “Come on,” she says, biting a painted lip. “I came here to help. Do you know how much trouble I saved you from the security systems, by cutting the camera feeds to half the facility? Bet you never stopped to think why security wasn’t all over your ass the moment you walked in here, did you? Why would I do all that just to fuck you over, huh?” “Well-” There’s a beep from the fallen Codex, and then a flash of blinding white light that sends you reeling, shielding your eyes with your weapon arm

We at Bright Water have many ways of turning the most barren field quite fertile.” As much as you’d like to see [rival.name] get what’s coming to [rival.himHer], you can’t help but observe you’re in the exact same position. “[rival.HeShe] can be an ass, but that hardly justifies all this,” you protest. Jaqui’s cybernetic eyes flash with a sudden storm of mad rage. “Theft? Betrayal? Urge not my wroth amiss, lest guilty of fault thy sweet self prove!” She blinks and her tone becomes soothing and bemused. “Your cousin thought to steal my research and sell it to one of your alien corporations. She’s clearly not been idle while you and Feruze were hemmed in by her endless waves of minions: red crystals are jammed into her body from every angle, jagged like they were just ripped from the pillar below and quivering in her body where they’re planted half into her shoulders, hips, and stomach. “You... you monsters. I won’t let you ruin my dream!” Corellah shouts, grabbing two of the largest crystals and pushing them deeper into her body, tearing huge gashes through her robe. “Even if I need to be a monster, too, to protect it.” She thrusts a hand forward, and the goos cavorting on the deck plating between you and Corellah part [silly|like the Red Sea|as though cut through by a knife]. You have just enough time to grab [rival.name] before the shockwave hits you, too, buffeting the pair of you to the side. Feruze dives for you, grabbing your hand before you can slide off the edge and pulling you back to your [pc.feet]. “Come on, quit laying around!” You grab your [pc.weapon] and turn to face the empowered Executress.
